description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a prominent gable roof that extends over the front and back facades. The front side includes a centered door flanked by two rectangular windows, all set beneath a modest roof overhang. The side views reveal the steep pitch of the roof and a single window on each gable end, contributing to a balanced and symmetrical appearance. The exterior walls are plain, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing functionality and straightforward design. The roof appears to be constructed from a single material, likely shingles or metal, and the windows are evenly spaced for a uniform look. The overall design suggests a compact, single-story residential building typical of suburban or rural neighborhoods.
description: A compact, single-story house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a centered front door. The design is simple and functional, with minimal exterior ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the centered door and two windows beneath the gable roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof and a single window on the gable end.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single centered window and plain wall beneath the roof overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other gable end, with a single window and steep roof pitch.
attribute_entryway_design: Centered single door with no porch, steps, or decorative elements, set beneath a simple roof overhang.
